description:
## What to build

Build the first issue explorer slice from the functional sidebar mockup. The app should render the three-pane shell and list real Beadwork issue data for the current workspace.

Use `bw list --all --json` through a Tauri command, normalize `null` to an empty issue list, and expose a narrowed `IssueSummary` contract to React. The current workspace is the directory Beadsmith was launched from and should be shown read-only.

Only listing all issues is in scope. Keep search, sidebar state filters, workspace switching, issue selection, and issue detail behavior inert or empty-state only. Add Lucide for icons now. Add shadcn components only if this implementation introduces reusable base components that need them; otherwise defer shadcn setup.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The app replaces the starter screen with the mockup-aligned issue explorer shell: left navigation, issue list pane, and right empty-detail pane.
- [ ] The issue list is populated from real `bw list --all --json` output in the launch current working directory.
- [ ] `null` or `[]` output from `bw` renders a true empty state, not an error.
- [ ] Missing `bw`, non-Beadwork directories, subprocess failures, and JSON parse failures render a visible error state while keeping the shell mounted.
- [ ] React receives a narrowed `IssueSummary` shape rather than the full Beadwork issue JSON.
- [ ] All Beadwork statuses are included; do not use the default actionable-only `bw list` behavior.
- [ ] Search, state filters, workspace switching, issue selection, and issue detail remain out of scope for behavior in this slice.
- [ ] The current workspace path is displayed read-only.
- [ ] Lucide icons are used for the shell iconography.
- [ ] shadcn is only added through the shadcn CLI if reusable base components are introduced in this slice.
- [ ] The implementation passes the repo's relevant frontend/Rust checks.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.

## Triage

ready-for-agent
title: List all issues in the issue explorer shell
